title: How cities are evaluating generative AI for public services
summary: A public-sector focused look at how agencies are testing generative AI for staff support, service delivery, and policy-controlled workflows.

title: Practical AI governance questions for local agencies
summary: Guidance-style coverage of the transparency, oversight, privacy, and procurement questions cities should ask before deploying AI tools.

title: Where AI can help public servants without replacing judgment
summary: A policy-centered overview of using AI for summarization, search, translation, and analysis while keeping humans accountable for decisions.

title: Local government cyber resilience starts with the basics
summary: A practical reminder that identity security, patching, backups, endpoint protection, and exercises remain essential for public-sector resilience.

title: Why ransomware planning matters for cities and counties
summary: Coverage of ransomware preparedness, incident response planning, data recovery, and the operational impact of attacks on local services.

title: Cybersecurity grant priorities for public agencies
summary: A state and local government perspective on strengthening cyber programs through planning, governance, shared services, and measurable controls.

title: GIS is becoming core civic infrastructure
summary: Examples of how maps, spatial analysis, dashboards, and field data collection support planning, public works, and emergency response.

title: How location intelligence supports climate and resilience work
summary: A look at how governments use mapping to understand flooding, heat, assets, vulnerable populations, and long-term infrastructure risks.

title: Open data maps help residents understand city services
summary: Coverage of public dashboards and map-based storytelling that make budgets, permits, projects, and service requests easier to understand.

title: Next Generation 911 moves emergency calling onto modern networks
summary: An overview of NG911 concepts, including IP-based routing, richer data, interoperability, and the operational changes facing emergency centers.

title: What public safety leaders should know about NG911 transition planning
summary: A planning-focused article on governance, standards, GIS data readiness, cybersecurity, training, and interagency coordination for NG911.

title: Emergency call centers prepare for data-rich requests for help
summary: Industry coverage of how emergency communications centers are preparing for texts, images, location data, and new operational workflows.

title: Land mobile radio remains essential for emergency responders
summary: A public safety communications perspective on why resilient radio coverage, priority access, and interoperability continue to matter.

title: Interoperability lessons for public safety networks
summary: Coverage of broadband, radio, dispatch, and mutual-aid coordination needs that help responders communicate during complex incidents.

title: Dispatch technology modernization reaches beyond the radio console
summary: An article-style overview of CAD, logging recorders, mapping, analytics, cybersecurity, and staffing considerations in communications centers.

title: Cloud modernization requires governance, not just migration
summary: A technology operations look at cloud cost controls, identity, backups, architecture, procurement, and service ownership for public agencies.

title: Hybrid infrastructure is the reality for many local governments
summary: Coverage of how cities balance legacy systems, SaaS tools, data centers, network reliability, and disaster recovery in hybrid environments.

title: Backups and continuity planning protect essential public services
summary: A resilience-focused article about recoverable backups, tested procedures, service prioritization, and continuity planning for agencies.

title: Smart infrastructure projects need clear public value
summary: A practical look at connected streetlights, sensors, traffic systems, and asset monitoring through the lens of outcomes and governance.

title: Digital twins and infrastructure planning move from pilots to practice
summary: Coverage of digital twin use cases for transportation, utilities, capital planning, resilience, and cross-department collaboration.

title: Sensor networks can improve operations when privacy is built in
summary: A civic technology discussion of responsible sensor deployments, data minimization, public communication, and measurable service benefits.

title: Digital government is a service design challenge
source: Beeck Center for Social Impact + Innovation
summary: An article on making digital services easier to use through plain language, accessibility, feedback loops, and cross-functional product teams.

title: Modernizing resident services without leaving people behind
summary: A people-centered view of forms, benefits, service portals, identity, accessibility, and the need to keep non-digital support available.

title: Procurement can make or break public-sector technology projects
summary: Coverage of modular procurement, user needs, delivery risks, vendor management, and sustainable ownership for government technology work.
